meta.title: Ulta AI CLI — Skill Consolidation Proposal
meta.description: 33 dirs → 12 skills: quality + redundancy report through the day-to-day Jira lens

props.title: 33 Skills → 12
props.subtitle: Quality + redundancy report, restructured around the actual day: Jira in, merged branch out
props.meta: Built from the 39-agent audit — no new scan needed

props.content: The audit graded skills individually; this report grades the SYSTEM. Verdict: the 33 directories encode ~12 real jobs. Five clusters are one job split across 3-6 dirs, three standards skills duplicate what .ulta/rules already owns, and the lifecycle entry point is wrong — the day starts with a Jira ticket, not a brainstorm.

props.label: THE DAY-TO-DAY LENS
props.title: One ticket, five stages, five skills
props.subtitle: debug and tdd are cross-cutting — entered from any stage, never scheduled

props.title: 🎫 Jira intake
props.content: Ticket lands. plan skill consumes it: loads .ulta/context first, parses acceptance criteria. Interview loop fires ONLY where the ticket is ambiguous — brainstorming demoted from front door to clarification mode.

props.title: 🧭 plan
props.content: Interview → repo research (patterns, prior art, LOE) → plan artifact with bite-sized tasks. One skill: today this is brainstorming + research-conductor + writing-plans, three dirs that only ever run as one loop.

props.title: ⚙️ execute
props.content: Plan in, options out: batch-with-checkpoints OR subagent-per-task OR direct. Worktree isolation is a STEP inside it, not a sibling skill. TDD governs every task.

props.title: 🔍 review
props.content: ONE review skill: checklist (what) + adversarial gates (how hard) + batch-audit mode + request/respond protocols. Repo-specific rules come from .ulta/rules — the per-repo layer that already exists.

props.title: ✅ finish
props.content: Verify with evidence → commit per repo standards → merge/PR options → cleanup own worktrees. Absorbs standards-commit and verification-before-completion; a commit-message skill is not a lifecycle stage.

props.label: REDUNDANCY
props.title: The five clusters
props.subtitle: Each row is one job currently split across multiple directories

rows.why: research-conductor has exactly one caller (brainstorming); writing-plans has exactly one feeder. They are phases of one loop, never independent.
Batch vs subagent is a MODE choice inside the same job; worktree setup is step 0 of either. Nobody invokes using-git-worktrees for its own sake.
Checklist/gates are co-injected by the PR pipeline (never separately); audit is batch mode; request/receive are 1-page etiquette protocols. Five dirs, one job.
root-cause-tracing IS phase 1 of systematic-debugging; defense-in-depth IS its fix pattern. The lane is one framework exploded into directories.
Verify → commit → merge/cleanup is one terminal sequence. A git-commit-message skill standing alone is the silliest split in the system.
Anti-patterns and flaky-test-waiting are TDD guardrails, not workflows. Appendices, not skills.
Both are content-generation sub-prompts with exactly one caller: onboarding.
Author → test → promote is one governance loop; promote is its last step.
Vendor-specific appendix of the same CI/hooks job. Also FIXES your complaint: automation should only INSTALL hooks that call review — today it spawns the whole standards web backwards.

props.label: DISSOLVE
props.title: standards-* are not skills — they are .ulta/rules with extra steps
standards-code / standards-testing / standards-documentation → delete after migrating content
props.content: All three read rule files that the repo-rules layer (.ulta/rules/) already owns — standards-code even reads a path onboarding never generates (03-code-style.md, broken today). The repo-specific review knowledge belongs in .ulta/rules where onboarding writes it and review reads it. Migrate any generic guidance into the review skill, then delete the three dirs. This kills the repo-automation → standards → review spaghetti you flagged.

props.label: FULL LEDGER
props.title: Every skill: quality, redundancy, fate
props.subtitle: Quality grades from the 39-agent audit (A = production-ready … C = rewrite/dissolve)

rows.redundancy: Entry router duplicates lifecycle chain already in init.js template; never loads repo context despite firing every session
Only production-ready skill; but repo-context + repo-rules are content generators ONLY it invokes
Never invoked except by repo-onboarding; pure sub-prompt
Never invoked except by repo-onboarding; pure sub-prompt
Always runs with research-conductor + writing-plans; the 3 form one loop. Not the entry point once Jira intake exists
ONLY caller is brainstorming; Gemini-specific APIs; cannot stand alone
Only consumes brainstorming output, only feeds execution; middle of one loop
Same job as SDD with different dispatch strategy; both need worktrees + TDD; user picks a mode, not a skill
Mode variant of executing-plans
Setup STEP, not a workflow; personal cruft (Jesse paths); only meaningful inside execution
Solid core; testing-anti-patterns + condition-based-waiting are its guardrails split into 3 dirs
Mock-hygiene appendix of TDD; never invoked alone
Flaky-test recipe with project-specific Lace example; appendix material
The 4-phase framework already subsumes the other debug skills as phases 1-2
IS phase 1 of systematic-debugging; npm-hardcoded script

Adversarial gates = the HOW-HARD; pipeline-hardcoded next to checklist; always co-injected

Sender etiquette; duplicates checklist inline (L63-89); orphan template
Recipient etiquette; persona oddities (safe-word); 1 page of substance

Merge/PR/cleanup options are real; commit standards + verification are its missing steps

Vendor-locked variant of what repo-automation installs; appendix material
Client-requested (CFBOLT-1952); genuinely distinct on-demand job
Verified lifecycle-gap; needs invoker + macOS fix; distinct reporting job

fate.text: KEEP — wire or demote to repo-local

rows.redundancy: Meta pair with promoting-skills = one governance loop (author->test->promote)
Playbook for one CLI command; the back half of writing-skills lifecycle

props.title: Lifecycle (5)
props.content: **plan** → **execute** → **review** → **finish**, routed by a slimmed **using-ulta** that loads .ulta/context BEFORE anything else. The Jira ticket is the entry; brainstorming is a mode.

props.title: Cross-cutting (2)
props.content: **debug** (4-phase, entered from any stage on any failure) and **tdd** (governs every implementation task, guardrails included).

props.title: Repo layer (2)
props.content: **repo-onboarding** (generates .ulta/context + .ulta/rules — the per-repo brain every other skill reads) and **repo-automation** (installs hooks/CI that call review; nothing more).

props.title: On-demand + meta (3)
props.content: **dep-audit** (client-requested, CFBOLT-1952), **weekly-status** (wire it or demote to repo-local), **skill-authoring** (author → test → promote).

props.label: BLAST RADIUS
props.title: What the merge has to rewire
props.subtitle: Small, known surface — the audit already mapped every hard wire

rows.change: review-checklist + review-gates → review (the only code-level hardcode in the system)
brainstorming → writing-plans → … becomes plan → execute → review → finish
Same rename + add the missing repo-context-first bootstrap
Regenerate stage→skill table for the 12; retire the 49-skill inventory claim
~30 skills:<name> strings remapped to the 12 (mechanical; CI skill-validation gate catches misses)
Absorb the useful guts of standards-code/testing/documentation before deleting them
